GK-TOM-001.name: Tomato
GK-TOM-001.subtitle: Hungry, productive and perfect for summer growing.

quickCare.Pruning: Remove side shoots on cordon types

GK-TOM-001.notes: Avoid irregular watering, which can contribute to fruit splitting or blossom end rot. Support stems before they become heavy.

seasonalActions.Spring: Pot on seedlings and harden off gradually.
seasonalActions.Summer: Feed, tie in stems and monitor watering.
seasonalActions.Autumn: Ripen remaining fruit indoors if needed.
seasonalActions.Winter: Clean pots and plan next season's variety.

problems: Blossom end rot is linked to inconsistent water uptake.
Blight risk increases in damp conditions.
Whitefly and aphids can build up under cover.

GK-FRN-001.name: Fern
GK-FRN-001.subtitle: Shade-loving foliage for calm, green corners.
GK-FRN-001.notes: Most ferns prefer consistent moisture and dislike drying out fully. Avoid harsh direct sun, especially through glass.

seasonalActions.Spring: Refresh compost top layer and remove old fronds.
seasonalActions.Summer: Keep moisture consistent in warm weather.

problems: Crispy leaf edges can indicate dry air or underwatering.
Pale fronds may mean too much light.
Root rot can happen if left sitting in water.
propagation: Keep divisions shaded and moist while they settle.

GK-LAV-001.name: Lavender
GK-LAV-001.subtitle: Scented, sun-loving, pollinator friendly.
GK-LAV-001.notes: Do not overwater. Lavender dislikes sitting in wet compost. Add grit or perlite if the potting mix is too rich or heavy.
